steps.title: Spot the past time marker
steps.content: The phrase “by the end of the 1990s” anchors the sentence in a specific moment in the past, establishing when the organization's membership change occurred.

steps.title: Apply the past perfect rule
steps.content:
Had doubled correctly shows the membership doubling was finished by that past reference point—this is the past perfect tense, needed when an action is completed before a specific past time.

The present perfect doesn't work with past time markers, while present and future tenses contradict the timeframe.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
